refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms for teleoperation data streams.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to reduce effective packet loss.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control to adjust transmission parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms for teleoperation data streams.

potential_sources: Interaction between adaptive bitrate control and error correction algorithms.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality control within teleoperation data streams, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may not respond quickly enough to sudden network degradation.
Risk of increased latency due to error correction overhead.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time network condition monitoring to enhance adaptive algorithm responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and data stream processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and packet loss statistics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and control inputs.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control, poor containment for communication logic.
High containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication; low diffusion risk.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link reliability outside Teleoperation module control.
Risk of interface overload if Teleoperation module handles excessive communication responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface load and consider offloading network condition monitoring if complexity increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by implementing adaptive bitrate and error correction to meet packet loss requirements.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as communication logic is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor Teleoperation module, supporting clear ownership and architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring packet loss via Telemetry interface during teleoperation sessions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add real-time network condition monitoring to improve adaptive algorithm responsiveness.
Review interface load on Teleoperation module to avoid bottlenecks.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and interface criteria. Verification and ownership are well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs for further detail.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can handle communication quality management without excessive interface load.
open_risks: Adaptive algorithms may not respond quickly enough to sudden network degradation.
Potential latency increase due to error correction overhead.
evidence_needed: Performance data on adaptive bitrate and error correction effectiveness under varying network conditions.
Telemetry data confirming packet loss compliance during teleoperation.
